library.name: Basic Usage Contrast
library.description: Contrast-focused library for high-frequency words and structures that learners often confuse in context.

items.title: because / since / as
items.brief: Use this card to distinguish direct reasons from background or lighter reasons in common writing and speaking.

items.title: because / because of
items.brief: Use this card to choose correctly between a full clause and a noun phrase when giving a reason.
contrastHints: due to / because of / thanks to

items.title: therefore / so / that's why
items.brief: Use this card to compare spoken and formal ways of expressing a result after a reason.
contrastHints: due to / because of / thanks to

items.title: due to / because of / thanks to
items.pattern: due to / because of / thanks to + noun
items.brief: Use this card to choose between neutral, formal, and positive cause phrases.

items.title: although / though / even though
items.brief: Use this card to compare standard, lighter, and stronger concession in speaking and writing.

items.title: although / despite / in spite of
items.pattern: although + clause / despite + noun / in spite of + noun
items.brief: Use this card to choose between concessive clauses and concessive phrases without making form mistakes.

items.title: but / however / yet / still
items.brief: Use this card to compare the most common contrast connectors by tone, position, and degree of surprise.

items.title: while / whereas
items.brief: Use this card to compare side-by-side contrast in general writing and more formal analysis.

items.title: if / unless
items.brief: Use this card to compare ordinary conditions with negative conditions and avoid double-negative mistakes.
contrastHints: if / as long as / provided that

items.title: if / as long as / provided that
items.pattern: if / as long as / provided that + clause
items.brief: Use this card to compare ordinary conditions with stronger or more formal requirement conditions.

items.title: even if / even though
items.pattern: even if + clause / even though + clause
items.brief: Use this card to separate hypothetical concession from real concession.

items.title: in case / if
items.brief: Use this card to compare preparing for a possibility with stating an ordinary condition.
contrastHints: if / as long as / provided that

items.title: when / while
items.brief: Use this card to compare time points with overlapping actions or periods.

items.title: when / once / as soon as
items.pattern: when / once / as soon as + clause
items.brief: Use this card to compare a general time trigger with a one-time trigger and an immediate next step.

items.title: before / after / by the time
items.brief: Use this card to compare earlier, later, and deadline-like time reference in action sequences.

items.title: until / by the time
items.pattern: until + clause / by the time + clause
items.brief: Use this card to compare an action continuing up to an endpoint with a state already true by a later point.

items.title: that / whether / if (noun clause)
items.brief: Use this card to compare reported statements with yes-no uncertainty clauses.

items.title: what-clause / that-clause
items.brief: Use this card to compare clauses that include their own meaning slot with plain reported statement clauses.

items.title: whether or not / whether / if
items.pattern: whether or not / whether / if + clause
items.brief: Use this card to compare ordinary yes-no uncertainty with a stronger all-cases meaning.

items.title: who / which / that (relative clause)
items.brief: Use this card to compare the most common relative pronouns for people, things, and flexible everyday usage.

items.title: where / when / why (relative clause)
items.brief: Use this card to compare relative clauses about place, time, and reason.

items.title: so that / in order to
items.pattern: so that + clause / in order to + base
items.brief: Use this card to choose between a full purpose clause and a simpler to-infinitive purpose phrase.

items.title: too...to / enough to
items.pattern: too + adj + to / adj + enough to
items.brief: Use this card to compare when a degree blocks an action and when it makes an action possible.

items.title: rather than / instead of / would rather
items.pattern: rather than / instead of / would rather
items.brief: Use this card to compare neutral substitution with stronger personal preference between options.

items.title: used to / be used to / get used to
items.pattern: used to / be used to / get used to
items.brief: Use this card to compare past habits with present familiarity and the process of becoming familiar.
